questions.question: What is the process of conversion of water into its vapour state?
questions.explanation: The process by which water transforms from liquid to gas.

questions.question: What is the process of conversion of water vapour into its liquid state?
questions.explanation: The process by which water vapour transforms back into liquid water.

questions.question: In what states can water be found?
questions.explanation: Water can exist in three main states of matter: solid (ice), liquid (water), and gas (water vapour).

questions.question: What happens to water when it is heated or cooled?
questions.explanation: When water is heated, it changes from a liquid to a gas. When it is cooled, it changes from a gas back to a liquid.

questions.question: What are some conditions that can affect the rate of evaporation?
questions.explanation: The rate of evaporation can be influenced by factors such as the exposed surface area, humidity levels, and air movement.

questions.question: What is a cooling effect that occurs due to evaporation?
questions.explanation: When water evaporates, it takes heat away from its surroundings, resulting in a cooling effect.

questions.question: How does water vapour rise into the air?
questions.correct_answer: Because it is lighter than air
questions.explanation: Water vapour rises because it is less dense than air, causing it to float upwards.

questions.question: What happens when water droplets in clouds get too heavy?
questions.explanation: When water droplets in clouds become too heavy to remain suspended in the air, they fall to the ground as precipitation (rain).

questions.question: What is a type of cooling device that uses evaporation?
questions.explanation: A pot-in-pot cooler is a device that uses evaporation to cool its contents by placing one pot inside another with a gap between them.

questions.question: Why does water seep through the surface of an earthen pot?
questions.explanation: Water seeps through the surface of an earthen pot due to condensation, which is the process by which water vapour transforms back into liquid water.

important_notes.title: Evaporation and Condensation
important_notes.content: These are the two main processes that involve the transformation of water between its solid, liquid, and gas states.

important_notes.title: States of Water
important_notes.content: Water can exist in three main states: solid (ice), liquid (water), and gas (water vapour).

important_notes.title: Cooling Effect of Evaporation
important_notes.content: Evaporation causes a cooling effect due to the removal of heat from its surroundings.

important_notes.title: Condensation in Clouds
important_notes.content: Clouds form when water vapour condenses around dust particles, leading to the formation of droplets that can eventually fall as rain.

important_notes.title: Pot-in-Pot Cooler
important_notes.content: A pot-in-pot cooler uses evaporation to cool its contents by placing one pot inside another with a gap between them.

important_notes.title: Surahi
important_notes.content: A surahi is an earthen pot used to keep water cold in summers by using the cooling effect of evaporation.

important_notes.title: Evaporative Cooling in Nature
important_notes.content: Evaporation occurs naturally in various forms, such as when we sweat and the evaporation causes a cooling effect or when plants release water vapour into the air.

important_notes.title: Water Vapour Rising
important_notes.content: Water vapour rises because it is lighter than air, causing it to float upwards.

important_notes.title: Formation of Rain
important_notes.content: Rain forms when water droplets in clouds become too heavy to remain suspended in the air and fall to the ground as precipitation.

important_notes.title: Cooling Devices Using Evaporation
important_notes.content: There are various cooling devices that use evaporation, such as pot-in-pot coolers, surahis, and other earthen pots designed for cooling water or food.

facts.fact: The process of conversion of water into its vapour state is called evaporation.
The process of conversion of water vapour into its liquid state is called condensation.
Water can exist in three main states: solid (ice), liquid (water), and gas (water vapour).
Evaporation causes a cooling effect due to the removal of heat from its surroundings.
Clouds form when water vapour condenses around dust particles, leading to the formation of droplets that can eventually fall as rain.
A pot-in-pot cooler uses evaporation to cool its contents by placing one pot inside another with a gap between them.
A surahi is an earthen pot used to keep water cold in summers by using the cooling effect of evaporation.
Evaporative cooling occurs naturally when we sweat and the evaporation causes a cooling effect or when plants release water vapour into the air.
Water vapour rises because it is lighter than air, causing it to float upwards.
Rain forms when water droplets in clouds become too heavy to remain suspended in the air and fall to the ground as precipitation.
